text.title: Agent On Call Runbook Template Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of Agent On Call Runbook Template enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every field in the Agent On Call Runbook Template template is either filled, marked N/A with reason, or absent — no placeholder text in the delivered artefact.
rule.rationale: Placeholder text ('TBD', 'lorem ipsum') ships to clients and erodes trust. Required fields force decision before sign-off.

rule.statement: Template fields are named with their consumer in mind — every label maps to who reads it (client, contractor, executive, AI agent).

rationale.source: technical writing standards / Microsoft Manual of Style

rule.rationale: Internal-jargon labels create misreads. 'Stage gate' for the client should read 'Approval milestone'.

rule.statement: Versioning rule: any change to template field set bumps a version stamp on the template, AND each instance carries the template_version it was authored against.
rule.rationale: Field-set drift breaks downstream consumers (parsers, automations). Pinning the version restores compatibility.

rule.statement: Each template ships with at least one fully-worked good example beside it. Empty templates without an exemplar are not deliverable.
rule.rationale: Empty templates are interpreted differently by every user. A worked example is the spec.

example.title: Templated brief with anchored fields
example.code: Project: <name> Client: <legal entity> Scope: <one paragraph + bullet list of deliverables> Out of scope: <bullet list> Milestones: <date + acceptance criterion>
example.note: Each field directs the author to a specific decision. Empty fields are visible.

example.title: Free-form 'use this template' doc with placeholder prose
example.code: Project: TBD Client: TBD Scope: To be discussed
example.note: TBD ships to the client. The template adds zero discipline.
